shortDescription.text: Zip Slip - Path Traversal via Archive Extraction

fullDescription.text: Using zipfile.ZipFile.extractall() without validating archive entry paths allows crafted archives with entries like '../../etc/cron.d/evil' to write files outside the intended extraction directory, enabling arbitrary file overwrite or creation (including web shells, cron jobs, SSH keys, etc.).

shortDescription.text: Sensitive Path Information Disclosed in Response

fullDescription.text: The endpoint returns the full server-side paths of extracted files and the temporary extraction directory in the JSON response. This leaks internal filesystem layout to the caller.

shortDescription.text: No file type or size validation on uploaded archive

fullDescription.text: The endpoint accepts any uploaded file as a ZIP archive without validating MIME type, magic bytes, or content size. An attacker can upload a zip bomb or other malicious archive.

shortDescription.text: Temporary directory never cleaned up after extraction

fullDescription.text: tempfile.mkdtemp() creates a temporary directory that is never removed. Repeated requests accumulate extracted files on disk indefinitely, enabling denial of service through disk exhaustion.

message.text: zipfile.ZipFile.extractall() is called without validating or sanitising archive entry paths. An attacker can craft a ZIP file containing entries with path traversal sequences (e.g. '../../etc/passwd') to overwrite arbitrary files on the server filesystem, achieving RCE or persistent backdoor installation. Fix: iterate zip.namelist(), resolve each with os.path.realpath(), and reject any path that does not start with the extract directory prefix.

The JSON response includes 'extracted_files' (full absolute server paths) and 'extract_dir' (the temporary directory path). This discloses internal server filesystem layout and aids further exploitation via path traversal or local file inclusion.

The endpoint saves the uploaded file without any content-type, magic-byte, or size validation. An attacker can upload a zip bomb causing resource exhaustion, or an unexpected file format that triggers parsing vulnerabilities in the zipfile module.

snippet.text:
if 'archive' not in request.files:
        return jsonify({'error': 'No archive uploaded'}), 400

    archive = request.files['archive']

    # Create temporary directory
    extract_dir = tempfile.mkdtemp()

    try:
        # Save uploaded archive
        archive_path = os.path.join(extract_dir, 'archive.zip')

message.text: tempfile.mkdtemp() creates a temporary directory that is never removed after the request completes. There is no finally block calling shutil.rmtree(). Each request leaves extracted files on disk indefinitely, enabling disk exhaustion denial of service.

description.text: Flask API blueprint with Zip Slip vulnerability in extract_archive() endpoint
